macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Existing Home Sales Disappoint in April Amid High Mortgage Rates
items.summary: US existing home sales in April came in below expectations at an annualized rate of 4 million units, with unsold inventory rising to 1.35 million units and 16 weeks of supply due to high mortgage rates.

items.title: Economists Anticipate Rising April CPI Led by Energy Prices
items.summary: The upcoming April CPI report is expected to show significant inflation increases driven by gas and energy costs amid Middle East supply disruptions, while core inflation remains stable.

items.title: S&P 500 Gains 10% in April on Strong Earnings and AI Momentum
items.summary: US stocks hit records with S&P 500 up 10% in April, fueled by upward revisions in corporate earnings especially in mega-cap tech and AI revenues, despite high oil prices from Middle East tensions.

items.title: China April CPI Rises to +1.2% YoY with Producer Prices Turning Positive
items.summary: China's April CPI increased 1.2% year-on-year and fuel costs surged 4.6%, while producer prices rose 3.5% YoY for the first time since October 2022; vehicle sales were marginally lower than last year.

items.title: Fed Signals Caution on Rate Cuts Amid Persistent Inflation Risks
items.summary: The Fed held rates steady with weakened conviction for further cuts, citing risks from wage growth and core services inflation; ECB signaled potential hikes ahead.

items.title: US Stock Market Faces Key Week with Inflation Data and Iran Tensions
items.summary: Wall Street enters a critical week monitoring inflation reports, consumer spending, and Iran conflict developments amid resilient labor market from recent strong jobs data.

items.title: Bitcoin Price at $81,983 Up 0.6% with Low Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at US$81,983, up 0.6% from yesterday with 24-hour volatility under +/-1.4%.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Circle Stock Surges After $222M Raise Led by BlackRock for Arc Blockchain
items.summary: Circle Internet Group raised $222 million in presale for its institutional Layer 1 blockchain Arc from investors including BlackRock, ARK Invest, and a16z, at a $3B valuation; stock rose 2% to $116.23 amid Q1 earnings with $694M revenue and 28% USDC circulation growth.

items.title: Over $163M in Crypto Long Positions Liquidated in 24 Hours
items.summary: More than $163 million in long positions were liquidated across exchanges, primarily Bitcoin and Ethereum futures, amid market declines; follows $253M long liquidations on May 8.

items.title: Over $163M in Crypto Long Positions Liquidated in 24 Hours
items.summary: Crypto markets saw over $163M in long position liquidations in 24 hours, highlighting ongoing leverage-driven selloffs in 2026.

items.title: Bitcoin Falls Below $81K Amid Iran-US Tensions and Market Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped below $81K after failing to hold breakout levels, with markets eyeing Iran-US risks, oil shocks, and upcoming events like CPI and CLARITY Act vote; TON and Zcash surge.

items.title: XRP Breaks $1.45 Resistance on Heavy Volume
items.summary: XRP surged from $1.4176 to $1.5073, breaking $1.45 resistance with high volume, outpacing Bitcoin in a 6.5% 24-hour range.

items.title: Dormant Bitcoin Whale Moves $41M BTC After 12 Years
items.summary: A bitcoin wallet dormant for 12 years transferred 500 BTC worth $41M on May 10, amid patterns of whale movements preceding exchange deposits; BTC near $80,900.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Disrupts AI-Driven Cyberattack Exploiting Zero-Day Vulnerability
items.summary: Google revealed it thwarted a criminal group's use of an AI large language model to discover and exploit a previously unknown zero-day vulnerability in a company's online system administration tool, notifying the affected firm and law enforcement before any damage occurred.

items.title: Google Halts Hackers Using AI to Target Unknown Digital Weakness
items.summary: Google's threat intelligence team disrupted prominent threat actors employing AI to bypass two-factor authentication via a zero-day exploit, highlighting growing cybersecurity risks from AI-powered hacking not linked to Google's Gemini or Anthropic's Claude Mythos.

items.title: Anthropic's Mythos AI Model Sparks Cybersecurity Panic and Regulation Calls
items.summary: Anthropic's advanced Mythos model, noted for exceptional hacking and cybersecurity capabilities, is limited to trusted organizations, prompting US government evaluations of top AI models from Google, Microsoft, xAI, and others amid mixed Trump administration signals.

items.title: Anthropic Partners with SpaceX for Massive Compute Capacity
items.summary: Anthropic announced SpaceX will provide 300 megawatts of computing power using over 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs by month's end, with interest in space-based AI data centers; the deal boosts Anthropic's rate limits amid surging demand.

items.title: Meta Poaches Four More Researchers from OpenAI
items.summary: Meta has hired OpenAI researchers Shengjia Zhao, Jiahui Yu, Shuchao Bi, and Hongyu Ren, continuing its talent acquisition from the rival AI firm.

items.title: China's AI Rise Challenges US Chip Controls Under Trump
items.summary: China's rapid AI progress, including DeepSeek's shift to Huawei chips, questions the efficacy of US export restrictions on Nvidia tech, forcing Trump to consider escalation or relaxation amid Beijing-Washington tech rivalry.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Farming Simulator 25 Announces Freightliner, Deberti, Axial Flow 260 and More
items.summary: New content update for Farming Simulator 25 revealed, including Freightliner trucks, Deberti equipment, and the Axial Flow 260 combine harvester.

items.title: Backrooms Horror Video Game Releasing Next Week on Consoles
items.summary: The Backrooms, a new liminal horror game from Puppet Combo, launches May 14th on Xbox, PlayStation, and Nintendo Switch, but not on PC.

items.title: Longlegs Horror Sequel Announced for 2028
items.summary: A sequel to the horror film Longlegs is confirmed for release in 2028.

items.title: Indie Gaming Highlights: 11-17 May 2026 Features Gold Gold Adventure and Mad King Redemption
items.summary: Weekly indie game roundup showcases upcoming titles like Gold Gold Adventure and Mad King Redemption, along with other releases for the week.

items.title: Bungie Faces Challenges but Creator Believes It Can Be Saved with Marathon and Destiny Strategies
items.summary: Analysis of Bungie's position using Steam charts and Sony reports suggests paths to recovery for Marathon and Destiny 2 amid industry difficulties.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Stablecoins Transforming Global Remittances for Filipinos
items.summary: Filipinos working overseas send $35 billion home annually, with a growing portion using stablecoins for cheaper, near-instant cross-border payments. The GENIUS Act and bank interest are accelerating stablecoin adoption despite their small current share of global payments.

items.title: Amerigo Scientific Launches Lipid-Based Transfection Reagents for Stem Cell Research
items.summary: Amerigo Scientific has introduced a new portfolio of advanced lipid-based transfection reagents designed specifically to enhance stem cell research and engineering.
